what_this_pass_proved: Star ratings (★★★ zero misses, ★★ ≤2 misses with ≥50% correct, ★ otherwise) give the player a visible mastery target beyond just surviving all 15 rounds.
Session debrief showing correct/close/miss counts, average precision in degrees, and breakdown by prompt type (degrees vs radians vs coordinates) turns the end screen into diagnostic feedback.
Contextual takeaway logic (diagnoses timeouts, worst prompt type, or precision issues) gives specific advice rather than generic encouragement — the player leaves knowing what to practice.
Angle-type breakdown reveals whether the player struggles with degrees, radians, or coordinates specifically — this was invisible before.
open_questions: Are the star thresholds correctly tuned? ★★★ requiring zero misses across 15 rounds may be too punishing for early players.
Does the prompt-type breakdown change behavior? Does a player who sees 'worst: coordinates' actually practice coordinates next run?
Is average precision in degrees meaningful feedback, or is it too abstract for the target age group?

bugs_fixed.description: No end-of-session feedback existed — game ended with just correct/total count. Added star rating, precision stats, prompt-type breakdown, and contextual takeaway.

known_limits: Star rating is session-aggregate. Does not show per-tier performance, so a player who aces Tier 1 but fails Tier 3 gets a blended rating.
Debrief persistence is session-only. No cross-session tracking of precision improvement.
Takeaway logic uses simple heuristics (worst prompt type, timeout count). May misdiagnose when multiple issues overlap.
next_pass_first_obligation: Verify that the star rating creates a replay pull — does a ★★ player want to try again for ★★★, or do they just close the game?
